name: French Market Doughnuts
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large bowl, sprinkle yeast over warm water; stir until dissolved.
2. Add sugar, salt, eggs, and milk to the yeast mixture. Blend with a beater.
3. Gradually add 4 cups of flour, beating smooth after each addition.
4. Add shortening and beat in the remaining flour.
5. Cover with plastic wrap and chill overnight.
6. Roll out on a floured board to an 1/8-inch thickness.
7. Cut into 2-1/2-inch squares.
8. Deep fry at 360°F for 2–3 minutes until lightly browned on each side.
9. Drain on paper towels and sprinkle heavily with powdered sugar.
10. Serve hot with café au lait.
Note: This dough can be kept up to a week in the refrigerator and actually improves with age; simply punch down when it rises. The dough can also be frozen; thaw, cut and roll, or shape donuts before freezing. It tastes like the original!
